notes:
After adding this line of code: PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command% on the launch options and then the game works.

I tried the in-game continue option and i got right back were i was.

I also tried to play the original 2014 edition and that just gives an error and doesn't start.

However, CPU usage is higher then on Windows. i got an average of around 50-80%
And there was audio stuttering.

When i beat the boss, there was a huge lag spike due to the amount of particles.
probably due to the computer i'm using (It's a Laptop from 2014)

Note: You have to use the pause menu to quit the game, i used alt+f4 once and the game crashed with a lot of errors.

Due to that it gets a Silver Rating.
title: They Breathe

notes:
Game started with no problems out of the box.
I had to quit the game for a few minutes and the continue function worked with no problems.

I had some audio crackling issues and it was a little annoying, but it didn´t stop me from playing the game. (This is probably happening due to my terrible integrated HD audio)

CPU usage was around 15-20% but that´s ok. (Proton conversion magic probably?)
played it with no hassle. Deserves an easy platinum!
title: They Breathe

notes:
I recently switched my OS to check if i could get better support for this game, and yes! this games works out of the box on Solus!

I had some audio issues, but it´s working flawless.

CPU usage was a solid 50% on all cores. which is a lot better then the directx to opengl conversion stuff.
This is probably due to having the latest mesa drivers (Solus OS is a rolling distro i think?)

still a silver, even due to audio issues and reinstalling another OS.
title: They Breathe
